capture_context.scope: Android → iPhone advert-only legacy-beacon receive on hardware
capture_context.android_app: dev.mob.mob (existing build with BleSelfTest periodic dispatch, ~7 s cadence)
notes: This is the canonical proof that the new MessageAdvertisementObserver MB branch reaches the harness delegate on real iOS hardware with a real Android counterparty.

Production app bridge (MobNativeBLEBridge + MobBLEClient) also already routes legacy beacon observation through mobDidObserveLegacyBeacon → mob_ble_emit_received_message_beacon NIF; the harness path is the test/dev surface for the same wire shape.

android_secondary.name: ambient — not on adb
android_secondary.note: Independent Android peer in BLE range; identified by stable sender_peer_id_hash. Not adb-attached during this capture.

outcome.device_id_rotation_reason: iOS CoreBluetooth privacy MAC rotation on observation; stable identity is sender_peer_id_hash

what_this_proves: iOS MessageAdvertisementObserver decodes Android-emitted 22-byte MB legacy beacons byte-for-byte at the harness layer.
Cross-platform identity (sender_peer_id_hash) round-trips correctly between Android base64 and iPhone hex.
Two independent Android peers were decoded simultaneously, confirming multi-peer scanning works.

MobLegacyBeaconAdvertisement.parse(manufacturerData:) accepts the same byte layout that Android's MobMessageAdvertisement legacy beacon path emits — this corroborates the static cross-platform fixture test in MessageAdvertisementTests.swift (commit ec09551) with live radio.
what_this_does_not_prove: iPhone → Android receive: the Android app's MeshX-level scanner was not running in this capture (only BleSelfTest dispatch was active). The Android receive path on shipping app code is a separate follow-up.
Full MX envelope (extended-advertising) receive on iOS production bridge: still an open gap (see docs/BLE_BRIDGE.md iOS parity gaps).
